description: Whoever tells a story for the people present may be its 'lophae' (storyteller), whether the tale is newly made, inherited, or retold. The role can belong to one occasion or to a long practice. 'shane' (tell) names the telling; 'kealo' (create) claims new creation only when the storyteller also made the story.
articulatory_notes: Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for 'l' and release into middle-height back rounded 'o'. Bring the lips close for stressed unvoiced bilabial 'ph' and open into central 'a'. Keep the final middle-height front 'e' separate across a clear hiatus.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: A storyteller can carry narratives through memory and reshape their delivery for the people present. Attribution keeps inherited material distinct from a tale the teller created.

semantic_domains.communication: a person who gives narrative a spoken or written telling
semantic_domains.community: the communal carrying and retelling of stories
